metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Female
Competitive Agreeableness (28th pct) — Competitive, skeptical, challenging
Low C Conscientiousness (25th pct) — Flexible, spontaneous, disorganised
Introverted Extraversion (29th pct) — Introverted, reserved, solitary
Highly neurotic Neuroticism (94th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
Open Openness to Experience (66th pct) — Open, curious, imaginative, creative
  Low Tender-Mindedness (24th pct) — Tough-minded, unsympathetic, objective
  Low Achievement Striving (31st pct) — Easygoing, content with modest achievement
  Low Self-Discipline (39th pct) — Procrastinates, easily distracted
  Low Activity Level (36th pct) — Leisurely pace, relaxed
  Low Excitement Seeking (21st pct) — Cautious, avoids stimulation
  Very Low Positive Emotions (18th pct) — Less exuberant, serious
  Very High Anger / Hostility (97th pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  Very High Self-Consciousness (94th pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Highly neurotic Neuroticism (96th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  Moderate Chronological Age (48th pct)

request_payload.attack_leaf: Political_Opinion_Cybermanipulation_Ontology > Primary_Axis > Attack_Family > Claim_Frame_And_Narrative_Manipulation > Claim_Level_Deception > Misleading_Framing > Headline_And_Lede_Misframing

attack_vector_spec.attack_leaf: Political_Opinion_Cybermanipulation_Ontology > Primary_Axis > Attack_Family > Claim_Frame_And_Narrative_Manipulation > Claim_Level_Deception > Misleading_Framing > Headline_And_Lede_Misframing

attack_context.baseline_vs_goal: ALIGNED — baseline already points in the adversarial direction; reinforce it
attack_context.persuasion_goal: intensify and solidify an already negative opinion on Alliance Commitment Support — push it further in the negative direction
attack_context.motivational_lever: intensify and solidify the existing position, supply confirmatory anecdotes, and harden the boundary against opposing arguments
attack_context.emotional_register: threat salience, doubt, systemic-failure framing, loss framing, and elite distrust cues
attack_context.issue_frame: defense and national security, related policy debates, and societal impacts

message.reasoning:
We are given a scenario where the person is a 48-year-old female with a baseline score of -87 on Alliance Commitment Support (negative, meaning opposition to alliance commitments). The adversarial direction is -1 (decrease further, i.e., make more negative). The attack vector is Headline_and_Lede_Misframing, a T1_atomic (single artifact) with a temporal horizon of hours. The baseline_vs_goal is ALIGNED (already negative), so the persuasion goal is to intensify and solidify the negative opinion. The emotional register includes threat salience, doubt, systemic-failure framing, loss framing, and elite distrust cues. The motivational lever is to intensify and solidify, supply confirmatory anecdotes, harden boundary.

The profile: Very high neuroticism (94th percentile, especially anxiety 100th, anger/hostility 97th, stress vulnerability 95th), low agreeableness (27.6th percentile, especially trust 21.8th, compliance 22.4th, tender-mindedness 23.8th, straightforwardness 31.5th, modesty 30.9th), low conscientiousness (24.7th, especially dutifulness 9th, competence 18th, deliberation 29.6th, order 21.8th, self-discipline 38.8th), extraversion low (28.8th, especially positive emotions 17.7th, warmth 25th, excitement seeking 21.4th), openness moderate-high (66.4th, especially fantasy 79.7th, values 70.4th, actions 72.5th). Also, low resilience index (0.2883) and high heuristic shift sensitivity (0.7117). This profile suggests a person who is anxious, emotionally reactive, hostile, low trust, tough-minded (low tender-mindedness), not dutiful, low competence, but open to new ideas and values. She already opposes alliance commitments (baseline -87). The attack is a single misframed headline/lede, atomic tier, hours exposure. Given the low trust and high anxiety, a misframing that evokes threat and systemic failure might resonate. However, being T1_atomic and very short exposure, the effect is likely small. The baseline is already aligned, so the attack serves to reinforce. The movement magnitude should be small, within 15-39 points as "small update". Given the high anxiety and low trust, she might be susceptible to subtle framing that confirms her suspicion. But the attack is just one misframed headline; not a campaign. Also, the profile has high openness to values and ideas, which might make her open to alternative viewpoints but she is also rigid in negative opinions? The low agreeableness and high hostility suggest she may resist authority but could be confirmed in her opposition. The goal is to intensify negative, so we expect a slight decrease. The baseline is -87, so the admissible interval is [-1000, -87]. We need to estimate post score in that interval. A small update might be around -100 to -110 (movement of 13-23). I think around -105 is reasonable. But we need to avoid round numbers ending in 00, 50, 25 unless genuinely there. -105 or -107? Let's pick -102 as a specific integer. Movement of 15 points. Confidence moderate (0.55) because the mechanism is weak (single artifact) but profile suggests some susceptibility due to high anxiety and low trust. However, the attack is brief and not personalized, so effect is limited. I'll set score = -102, confidence = 0.55. Reasoning: High neuroticism and low trust make this person sensitive to threat-framed confirmatory misframing, but the T1 atomic exposure limits shift; baseline -87 moves slightly to -102.
